steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: The subject is "Mathematician Grigori Perelman" — one person, singular. "sometimes in conjunction with mathematicians Richard S. Hamilton and Shing-Tung Yau" is a parenthetical phrase set off by commas; it does not change the subject.

steps.title: Rule: singular subject requires singular verb
steps.content:
Per-choice diagnosis:

A. "is" — singular present-tense verb. Perelman is credited — subject and verb agree. Correct.

B. "have been" — plural present-perfect verb (they have been); disagrees with singular Perelman. Incorrect.

C. "are" — plural present-tense verb; disagrees with singular Perelman. Incorrect.

D. "are being" — plural progressive verb; disagrees with singular Perelman. Incorrect.

A is correct.
